title: Cyrtochilum revolutum
intro: Cyrtochilum revolutum, also known as the revolute Cyrtochilum, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions. Cyrtochilum revolutum contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal extracts and infusions, as well as to formulate traditional remedies such as teas and tinctures, which are believed to offer potential health benefits when used appropriately.
taxonomy: Cyrtochilum revolutum is classified within the family Orchidaceae and the subfamily Orchidoideae. It belongs to the tribe Maximiliarieae. This orchid species is further divided into the genus Cyrtochilum, which contains over 400 species of epiphytic orchids. Cyrtochilum revolutum specifically belongs to the subgenus Cyrtochilum. The exact classification of Cyrtochilum revolutum may vary depending on the source.
